thread.title: Arizona’s tourism director resigns over reported conflict of interest
thread.title_full: Arizona’s tourism director resigns over reported conflict of interest

author: @KOLDNews
title: Arizona’s tourism director resigns over reported conflict of interest
text:
Arizona’s tourism director resigns over reported conflict of interest
PHOENIX (AZFamily) — Arizona’s top tourism official stepped down Friday morning amid reports of a conflict of interest involving redesigning the state logo.
Gov. Katie Hobbs said Arizona Office of Tourism director Lisa Urias resigned, but multiple reports say the resignation will be effective Dec. 4.
“I can say as a social worker, I take my role as a public servant very seriously and anyone who has the privilege of serving the public needs to be above reproach,” Hobbs said during a news conference. “This appearance of conflict is not acceptable.”
[According to the online political newsletter Arizona Agenda](https://www.arizonaagenda.com/p/the-bill-for-branding-arizona), the brother of the CEO of Urias’ marketing agency, Urias Communications, received $27,500 for the logo redesign. The entire project cost $700,000.
The report also alleges that Urias Communications was given a $250,000 contract by the Arizona Department of Education shortly after become tourism director. State officials are disputing that portion of the report.
“The document that has gained attention is not a contract at all, but a Competition Impractical document that was never approved. ADE has spent $15,225 with Urias for meeting planning services, but there has not been, nor will there be, a contract or expenditure for $250,000. Nor has any prior administration engaged in such a contract,” said Doug Nick, communication director for the Education Department.
Urias reportedly doesn’t have any role in the day-to-day operations at the firm.
Rep. Teresa Martinez, a Republican from Casa Grande, [called on Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes](https://www.azleg.gov/press/house/56LEG/2R/241121MARTINEZLTR.pdf) and Maricopa County Attorney Rachel Mitchell to investigate.
Senate president Warren Petersen [said on X, formerly known as Twitter](https://x.com/votewarren/status/1859762664991490335), there was zero chance Urias would be confirmed to her position.
“She should resign or be removed by the governor immediately,” he said.
It’s unclear who will take Urias’ place.
